rulings.Case Title: GREG BURGHART VS. DANIEL O'NEILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For The Appointment Of A Discovery Referee For The Supervision Of The Conduct Of Written Discovery, Depositions, And Related Activities
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANT DANIEL O'NEILL and CINDY O'NEILL's Second Amended Motion For The Appointment Of A Discovery Referee For The Supervision Of The Conduct Of Written Discovery, Depositions, And Related Activities GRANTED. The number of discovery motions and ex-parte applications in this case, where issue of lack of permits for the dwelling premises does not seem to be in dispute, constitutes extraordinary circumstances warranting appointment of referee. Additionally, transcripts of the depositions and parties arguments in regards to the depositions indicate additional discovery disputes will be presented to the Court. Parties should provide the Court with 3 nominations for referee. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: BARNABA BIENKOWSKI et al VS. LAWRENCE CHOY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANT CHRISTINA SUTTON'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Second Amended Compliant sufficiently alleges facts to support each cause of action alleged against moving defendant. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR ARREOLA et al VS. 1049 MARKET STREET LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANTs 1049 MARKET STRRET LLC, AMY BOGART, TERRY BOGART, JOHN GALL, RICHARD LANE, ROBERT MULLEN, HALLEY BOGART, SFOL MANAGEMENT COMPANY and SHI HO, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Defendant failed to meet the burden under the first prong as to causes of action 4, 7, 8, and 9, which are "based exclusively on allegations of habitability defects and decreased services" (Reply 5:20-23). Causes of action 2 and 3 are at least "mixed causes of action" in part based on non-protected activity. (Complaint Paragraphs 78-86; 94). Plaintiffs meet their burden to show probability of prevailing on the merits as to the implied breach of warranty of habitability and Defendant's violation of CC 1940.6. Wallace v. McCubin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK VS. RANDALL B. ALLISON et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2014, line 7. PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Plaintiff shifted its burden by presenting evidence establishing prima facie evidence of compliance with CC 2924 and CCP 1161a. See Nagamatsu Declarations and Trustee's Deed Upon Sale. Defendant failed to provide admissible evidence to create triable issue of fact regarding compliance and standing. = (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: BAY PROPERTY GROUP VS. OTGONTUYA JANCHIV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 17, 2014, line 10. DEFENDANT OTGONTUYA JANCHIV's AMENDED MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT DENIED. Court's judgment based on breach of 1(b) of the Stipulation for Entry of Future Judgment is supported by the facts presented to the Court on March 10, 2014. Specifically, parties entered into a stipulation; stipulation provided for payment of moneys on or before 5:00 p.m. on the first of the month; parties agreed that said payments were not rent of any kind; parties agreed that time was of the essence; defendant tendered payment on the 4th of the month by mail; under the stipulation plaintiff was entitled to judgment if defendant failed to comply with the terms of the stipulation. = (501/LKS)
